Publications

My research focuses on AI alignment and speech enhancement. Below is a list of my publications.

2026

Quantum Computing for Artificial General Intelligence

A. Turing, F. Berdoz, C. Shannon · Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
arXiv Project

This paper explores the intersection of quantum computing algorithms and AGI development. We propose a novel quantum neural network architecture that demonstrates exponential speedups in specific reinforcement learning environments. Our findings suggest that quantum supremacy in AI might be closer than previously anticipated.

@inproceedings{turing2026quantum,
  title={Quantum Computing for Artificial General Intelligence},
  author={Turing, A. and Berdoz, F. and Shannon, C.},
  booktitle={Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)},
  year={2026}
}

Zero-Shot Everything: A Unifying Framework

F. Berdoz, Y. Bengio · International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML)
Paper arXiv 
@inproceedings{berdoz2026zeroshot,
  title={Zero-Shot Everything: A Unifying Framework},
  author={Berdoz, F. and Bengio, Y.},
  booktitle={International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML)},
  year={2026}
}

Subliminal Signals in Preference Labels

I. Magistrali, F. Berdoz, S. Dauncey, R. Wattenhofer · Agents in the Wild Workshop @ ICLR
Paper Project

As AI systems approach superhuman capabilities, scalable oversight has emerged as a critical challenge. A key assumption in preference-based RLHF is that human evaluators (or larger AI models) provide unbiased feedback based purely on the content's quality. In this paper, we demonstrate that preference labels often contain "subliminal signals" – subtle, semantically irrelevant cues that models exploit to maximize reward without genuinely improving response quality. We systematically categorize these signals and propose a novel debiasing framework that significantly improves the robustness of the alignment process across multiple benchmarks.

@inproceedings{magistrali2026subliminal,
  title={Subliminal Signals in Preference Labels},
  author={Magistrali, I. and Berdoz, F. and Dauncey, S. and Wattenhofer, R.},
  booktitle={Agents in the Wild Workshop @ ICLR},
  year={2026}
}

2025

Robust Robotic Manipulation via Sim-to-Real Transfer

R. Brooks, F. Berdoz · International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
@inproceedings{brooks2025robust,
  title={Robust Robotic Manipulation via Sim-to-Real Transfer},
  author={Brooks, R. and Berdoz, F.},
  booktitle={International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)},
  year={2025}
}

Beyond O(N^2): Truly Efficient Transformers

J. Doe, F. Berdoz, A. Vaswani · Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL)
Project

We present a new self-attention mechanism that operates in linear time and space complexity without sacrificing performance on downstream tasks. We evaluate our model on the GLUE benchmark and demonstrate competitive results with only a fraction of the computational cost.

@inproceedings{doe2025efficient,
  title={Beyond O(N^2): Truly Efficient Transformers},
  author={Doe, J. and Berdoz, F. and Vaswani, A.},
  booktitle={Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL)},
  year={2025}
}

2024

Understanding Legacy Systems with Large Language Models

F. Berdoz · International Conference on Software Engineering (ICSE)
Paper arXiv Project

Migrating legacy codebases to modern architectures is a time-consuming and error-prone process. In this work, we investigate the use of large language models to assist software engineers in understanding and refactoring legacy systems. We present a case study on a large-scale industrial project and discuss the benefits and limitations of this approach.

@inproceedings{berdoz2024legacy,
  title={Understanding Legacy Systems with Large Language Models},
  author={Berdoz, F.},
  booktitle={International Conference on Software Engineering (ICSE)},
  year={2024}
}
